# Vegetable Garden / Urban Horticulture Site — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-vegetable-garden-urban-veg-garden
**Vertical slug:** vegetable-garden
**VN-ID:** VN-AGR-HRT-001
**Niche family:** NF-AGR-HRT (standalone urban horticulture)
**Family role:** standalone
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Urban and peri-urban vegetable farming is a growing sector in Nigeria, driven by food security concerns, rising urban middle-class demand for fresh produce, and greenhouse technology adoption. Key models include: Community Supported Agriculture (CSA) subscription boxes (weekly/monthly), hotel and restaurant direct supply, farmers market stalls, and hydroponics/greenhouse farms. Plateau State (Jos) is Nigeria's vegetable basket for tomatoes, peppers, and leafy greens; Lagos peri-urban farms serve the metro market. FMARD registration and NAFDAC certification (for processed products) are key compliance requirements. WhatsApp subscription order is the primary B2C contact channel.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Urban household Mrs Eze, 38, Lekki**: Wants fresh, pesticide-reduced vegetables delivered weekly. Pays ₦8,000/month for mixed box. Subscribes via WhatsApp.
- **Hotel executive chef Emeka, 42, Abuja**: Sources tomatoes, peppers, leafy greens, and herbs weekly for 100-room hotel. Needs consistent supply, invoice, and bank transfer.
- **Restaurant owner Aisha, 35, Kano**: Sources organic vegetables for upscale restaurant. Wants FMARD-certified farm, regular delivery, and WhatsApp updates on availability.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home**: Hero (farm name, "Fresh From Our Garden to Your Table"), WhatsApp subscription CTA, CSA box options (Basic/Family/Hotel with ₦ pricing), seasonal produce calendar, trust strip (FMARD, CAC, organic certification if applicable)
- **About**: Farm story, growing practices (organic/low-pesticide), location (Jos, Lagos, Ibadan peri-urban), FMARD registration
- **Products**: Vegetable catalogue by season, CSA box plans with ₦ pricing, hotel/restaurant supply options
- **Contact**: WhatsApp subscription (primary), farm location, subscription/delivery form (NDPR-compliant)

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Fresh, farm-to-table, and health-forward. "Grown in Nigeria. Good for Nigeria", "Fresh Weekly Delivery", "Direct from Our Garden". Primary CTA: "Subscribe via WhatsApp". Secondary: "View Seasonal Produce". Avoid: generic western organic farm imagery.

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- FMARD farm registration
- CAC business registration
- Organic/low-pesticide growing practice statement
- CSA subscription member count badge
- Hotel/restaurant supply references
- Fresh delivery guarantee
- Seasonal availability calendar

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Vegetable garden — Nigerian farm worker harvesting tomatoes, Plateau State/Jos setting
2. Weekly CSA box — mixed vegetables in basket, fresh and colourful, Nigerian household setting
3. Greenhouse rows — neat vegetable rows, Nigerian urban farm, modern setup
4. Farmers market stall — fresh produce display, Nigerian Saturday market, price tags in ₦
5. Hotel delivery — organised produce crates being accepted by chef, Abuja hotel context
6. Hydroponic setup — vertical garden, urban rooftop, Lagos apartment building context
7. Customer unboxing — Nigerian family unboxing weekly vegetable subscription, fresh produce

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- [ ] FMARD farm registration
- [ ] CAC business registration number
- [ ] Pesticide usage compliance statement
- [ ] Organic certification (if claimed — from OFAB Nigeria or equivalent)
- [ ] NDPR consent on subscription form
- [ ] Delivery compliance (cold chain if applicable)

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp subscription CTA prominent
- [x] FMARD/CAC trust signals
- [x] Nigerian vegetables referenced (tomatoes, peppers, ugwu, waterleaf, garden egg)
- [x] ₦ subscription/delivery pricing
- [x] Nigerian farming location references (Jos, Lagos peri-urban, Ibadan)
- [x] Mobile-first layout
- [x] esc() on all inputs
- [x] NDPR on subscription form
